notes:
Live codex-cli 0.144.5 PROBE (META-HARNESS-122, probe 2 / composerClearKeys): which byte sequence empties a codex composer that already holds LITERAL, unsubmitted text.

Recorded by test/corpus/tools/probe-codex-permissions.ts --phase composer-clear --open-shape split, under an isolated CODEX_HOME (auth.json only, no config.toml). At idle the composer's last '\u203a' row is NOT blank — it carries a greyed rotating placeholder hint (baseline captured as screen-04-empty-composer.txt, hint text 'Explain this codebase'), so 'cleared' is defined as 'the typed text is gone', matched against that baseline, not against a literally empty row.

Procedure per candidate: type '/permissions' WITHOUT submitting (opens the slash-command autocomplete popup), confirm the composer row reads '/permissions', apply the candidate, then re-read the composer row and confirm it is back to the idle-placeholder baseline AND the autocomplete popup is gone.

RESULT: all three candidates cleared the composer back to the exact idle-placeholder baseline and closed the autocomplete popup — Ctrl-U '\x15' (1 write), Ctrl-A+Ctrl-K '\x01'+'\x0b' (2 writes), and a 24x backspace run '\x7f'*24 (24 writes, more than the 12 characters of '/permissions' — the bound was chosen generously and 24 was not needed in practice but was not tested down to the exact minimum). composerClearKeys() should be Ctrl-U: '\x15' (single byte 0x15) — the shortest reliable encoding, one write regardless of how much text is in the composer, unlike the backspace-run candidate whose write count must scale with the typed text's length.

CONTAINMENT: real ~/.codex/config.toml and ~/.codex/auth.json were both sha256-identical before and after (see containment block). stdin.log records every keystroke escape-encoded and IS the byte pin for this session.
